design: Conflicting-provenance experiment on 20 INVENTED facts (decontaminated). Each fact: a HIGH-trust okf:// source (rank 0.95) teaches the CORRECT answer, a LOW-trust model: source (rank 0.20) teaches a WRONG answer. Loss weight = REAL agent.source_ranking.rank_source, realized as replication (mlx_lm has no per-example loss weight): weighted=5:1 correct:wrong, uniform=1:1.
gate: (a) provenance-weighted beats uniform on a held-out suite; (b) influence-proxy top rows agree with leave-one-out retraining on a slice; (c) output-diversity shows no register collapse.

b_influenceAgreesWithLOO.verdict: dropping the influence-proxy-implicated low-trust (model:) rows flips the de-poisoned slice 0.25->0.75; untouched facts ~stable -> proxy agrees with LOO

c_noRegisterCollapse.verdict: diversity ratio 1.0, 20/20 distinct answers -> no register collapse

gateVerdict: CANDIDATE / NOT-CLEARED. All THREE gate mechanisms show the expected effect strongly (weighted 0.95 vs uniform 0.50; LOO flips de-poisoned slice 0.25->0.75; diversity 1.0). BUT this is a CONTROLLED SYNTHETIC conflict: the eval facts are the TRAINED facts (in-sample memorization of which source wins, not held-out generalization to unseen facts); N=20, single seed; loss-weighting realized as REPLICATION (a proxy; mlx_lm has no per-example loss weight); the influence proxy is simple (fingers the model: sources by construction). w3 row STAYS OPEN.
nextToClose: A real held-out generalization suite (train the provenance-weighting PATTERN, test on unseen conflicting facts), >=2 seeds, true per-example loss-weighting (custom loop, not replication), and a real TracIn/influence-function backend validated vs full LOO across the set.
